title: melaleuca quadrifida subsp. seminuda
intro: Melaleuca quadrifida subsp. seminuda, also known as the 'Red Honey Myrtle', is a plant species within the myrtle family native to Western Australia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its essential oil, which contains compounds like limonene and alpha-terpineol, exhibiting antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antifungal properties. The plant is said to provide various health benefits, including soothing skin irritations, reducing stress and anxiety, and aiding in wound healing. Herbal preparations of the Red Honey Myrtle include its essential oil, which can be used topically or in aromatherapy, as well as infusions and tinctures made from its leaves and flowers.
